description: Figure 3 presents the particle-size distribution of the PREP-prepared Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni powder used for selective laser melting. The chart displays a histogram (blue bars, left y-axis: Volume %) representing the frequency distribution of particle sizes, alongside a cumulative distribution curve (red line, right y-axis: Cumulative Volume %). The particle sizes are plotted on a logarithmic x-axis scale. According to the caption and citation text, the powder exhibits a narrow size distribution primarily in the 15–53 μm range, with a median particle size (D50) of 35.69 μm. The distribution shows a single dominant peak characteristic of gas-atomized or PREP-produced spherical powders. The powder demonstrates excellent flowability with a Hall flow value of 14 s/50 g, making it suitable for SLM processing. Note that while the x-axis is labeled in millimeters (mm), the context indicates this represents micrometers (μm), consistent with typical metal powder specifications for additive manufacturing.
